mission.title: Install and Configure Hyperlight on Kubernetes
mission.description: Hyperlight is a lightweight Virtual Machine Manager (VMM) designed for embedding within applications, enabling the safe execution of untrusted code in micro virtual machines with minimal overhead. Installing Hyperlight allows developers to leverage its capabilities for creating secure and efficient environments for running untrusted code.

steps.title: Add Hyperlight as a Rust dependency
steps.description:
Hyperlight is a Rust library for creating micro-VMs. Add it to your Cargo.toml:
```toml
[dependencies]
hyperlight-host = "0.3.0"
```
Or clone the repository:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/hyperlight-dev/hyperlight.git
cd hyperlight
```

steps.title: Build the Hyperlight examples
steps.description:
Build and run the example guest:
```bash
cargo build --release
cargo run --example hello-world
```

steps.title: Run the test suite
steps.description:
Verify Hyperlight works on your system:
```bash
cargo test
```
Requires KVM on Linux or Windows Hypervisor Platform on Windows.

steps.title: Verify hypervisor support
steps.description:
Check that your system supports hardware virtualization:
```bash
# Linux (KVM)
ls /dev/kvm

# Or check for mshv
ls /dev/mshv
```

resolution.summary: Hyperlight is available as a Rust library for creating lightweight micro-VMs for secure code execution.

uninstall.title: Remove Hyperlight dependency
uninstall.description:
Remove `hyperlight-host` from your `Cargo.toml` and run:
```bash
cargo clean
```

upgrade.title: Update the dependency
upgrade.description:
Update the version in Cargo.toml and run:
```bash
cargo update
cargo build --release
```

troubleshooting.title: KVM not available
troubleshooting.description:
Ensure KVM is enabled:
```bash
sudo modprobe kvm
sudo modprobe kvm_intel  # or kvm_amd
ls /dev/kvm
```

troubleshooting.title: Build fails on macOS
troubleshooting.description: Hyperlight requires Linux (KVM) or Windows (WHP). macOS is not currently supported.

troubleshooting.title: Permission denied on /dev/kvm
troubleshooting.description:
Add your user to the kvm group:
```bash
sudo usermod -aG kvm $USER
```

prerequisites.description: Rust toolchain and KVM (Linux) or Windows Hypervisor Platform required. No Kubernetes cluster needed.
